items.title: CMS guidance: states must move Medicaid expansion adults to 6-month eligibility renewals starting Jan. 1, 2027
items.summary: CMS State Medicaid Director letter SMD #26-001 explains that Section 71107 of the Working Families Tax Cut law requires states to redetermine eligibility every 6 months (instead of annually) for most people in the Medicaid expansion adult group, beginning with renewals scheduled on or after Jan. 1, 2027. CMS describes two transition options for enrollees already on 12-month cycles and says states must submit a state plan amendment to implement the change.

items.title: HUD warns PHAs to plan for 2026 voucher funding constraints; 2026 HAP amounts expected in spring
items.summary: HUD's 2026 Budget Management Letter (issued Feb. 3, 2026) says PHAs should take immediate cost-saving steps if needed because 2026 renewal funding may not fully cover current Housing Choice Voucher costs for every agency. HUD says 2026 Housing Assistance Payments (HAP) funding enclosures will be provided in spring 2026 after reconciliation of 2025 Voucher Management System data, and it recommends measures like reducing payment standards or pausing new project-based voucher commitments to avoid terminating assistance.

items.title: Education Department outlines Workforce Pell rules effective July 1, 2026 (8-0 weeks; 150-99 hours; 70% completion/placement)
items.summary: A Department of Education presentation for the Federal Student Aid community says Workforce Pell eligibility begins July 1, 2026 for short-term workforce programs that are at least 8 weeks but less than 15 weeks and between 150-99 clock hours (or equivalent credits). The same materials describe minimum verified 70% completion (within 150% of normal time) and 70% job placement measured 180 days after completion for these programs.

items.title: Pell eligibility change: starting 2026-27, students can lose Pell if SAI is -90-0% of max award (example: $7,395 max -4,790 cutoff)
items.summary: Education Department guidance notes that a student is ineligible for a Pell Grant if their Student Aid Index (SAI) equals or exceeds twice the maximum Pell award amount, beginning with the 2026-27 FAFSA implementation. The presentation gives an example: if the maximum Pell Grant is $7,395, a student with an SAI of 14,790 would be ineligible.

items.title: WIC: FY 2026 fruit/veg cash benefit set at $26 (kids), $48 (preg/postpartum), $52 (breastfeeding); states must add frozen/canned/dried produce by April 2026
items.summary: USDA FNS says FY 2026 monthly WIC cash-value voucher/benefit amounts are $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants. FNS also reminds state agencies that WIC food package changes require authorizing at least one additional form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026.

items.title: IRS: 2026 filing season opened Jan. 26; EITC/ACTC refunds expected by March 2 (direct deposit), and Where's My Refund updates by Feb. 21
items.summary: The IRS says it began accepting 2025 tax-year returns on Jan. 26, 2026, and notes the April 15 filing deadline. The IRS also says most refunds that include the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) or Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) should be available by March 2, 2026 for taxpayers using direct deposit (and with no other issues), with Where's My Refund providing projected deposit dates for most early EITC/ACTC filers by Feb. 21.

items.title: SSA: 2.8% COLA for 2026; SSI increases begin Dec. 31, 2025 and Social Security increases begin January 2026
items.summary: SSA announced a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment for 2026 affecting about 75 million Americans, including nearly 71 million Social Security beneficiaries and nearly 7.5 million SSI recipients. SSA says Social Security benefits increase starting in January 2026 and SSI payments increase starting Dec. 31, 2025.

items.title: SSA fact sheet: 2026 SSI federal payment standard is $994/month (individual) and $1,491/month (couple)
items.summary: SSA's 2026 COLA fact sheet lists the maximum federal SSI payment standard as $994 per month for an individual and $1,491 per month for a couple. The same SSA fact sheet lists 2026 disability work thresholds of $1,690/month for non-blind SGA, $2,830/month for blind SGA, and $1,210/month for the trial work period.

items.title: HUD deadline: Apr. 14, 2026 to apply for HUD-VASH administrative fee funding
items.summary: HUD issued PIH Notice 2026-01 (Revised) on March 25, 2026, announcing $10 million in available funding for public housing agencies administering HUD-VASH to support leasing HUD-VASH vouchers. Applications must be received no later than midnight on April 14, 2026.

items.title: USDA SNAP food restriction waivers list includes 2026 start dates (e.g., Texas Apr. 1; Florida Apr. 20; Colorado Apr. 30)
items.summary: USDA's SNAP Food Restriction Waivers page lists multiple state implementation dates, including Texas starting 04/01/26 (restricts sweetened drinks and candy), Florida starting 04/20/26 (restricts soda, energy drinks, candy, and prepared desserts), and Colorado starting 04/30/26 (restricts soft drinks). The same USDA list also shows later approved waivers including Kansas starting 02/15/27 and Nevada starting 02/01/28.

items.title: SNAP FY 2026: maximum allotment is $298 (1 person) / $994 (4 people) in the 48 states + DC; minimum benefit is $24
items.summary: USDA's SNAP COLA tables list FY 2026 maximum monthly SNAP allotments of $298 for a 1-person household and $994 for a 4-person household in the 48 states and DC. The tables also show a $24 minimum monthly allotment for 1-2 person households in the 48 states and DC, effective Oct. 1, 2025 through Sept. 30, 2026.

items.title: CMS: For Oct. 2025 Medicaid/CHIP renewals, 71% renewed (49% ex parte) and 19% disenrolled (13% procedural)
items.summary: CMS Feb. 24, 2026 slides from an All-State Medicaid and CHIP call report that for people due for renewal in October 2025, 71% had coverage renewed (including 49% renewed on an ex parte basis). The same CMS slides report 19% were disenrolled (13% for procedural reasons) and 10% were pending at the end of the month.
